goals.goal: Add a temporal-smoothing option to the wild 'age over frames' page (rolling mean) to demonstrate how averaging tames single-frame wobble.
Deepen the ai-image-detection corpus/edge cases and consider a WebGPU fast-path where it helps; matrix pass complete (support=ok).
Offer a lighter multilingual option (e.g. a smaller multilingual CLIP or q4f16) as a NEW additive variant so mobile users can run it comfortably.
Add a cross-lingual image-search rung: embed a small gallery once, then query it with a phrase in any language.
Add a WebGPU execution-provider fast path for arctic-embed (honest WASM fallback retained) as an additive enhancement.
Consider a small labelled retrieval set on the A/B panel so the prefix's nDCG-style benefit is visible over a corpus, not just a pair.
Add an auto-chunking practical variant that splits inputs longer than the 1024-token window, summarizes each chunk, and stitches a final pass — for full-length articles.
Deeper mobile tap-target + focus-order audit on the sliders and segmented toggle at 360px to flip support from needs-review to ok with recorded evidence.
Add a BART-large-vs-DistilBART head-to-head demo that runs both summarizers on one article and diffs the outputs + timings, so the higher-quality claim is shown, not just stated.
Add a BEiT-vs-ViT occlusion-robustness curve: sweep the mask rate on one image and plot both models' top-1 confidence, directly testing whether masked pretraining buys robustness to missing patches.
Add a label-efficiency framing demo: show BEiT's self-supervised features as a frozen backbone for few-shot fine-tuning on user images (the practical payoff of self-supervised pretraining).
Add a 'streaming redaction' wild demo: mask PER/LOC spans live as the user types, entirely on-device, showing the privacy story.
Agent matrix pass for bert-ner: Read the mobile+desktop screenshots, confirm real output + tap targets + focus + dialogs, then flip support to ok (or unsupported+evidence) and enrich this critique.
Agent matrix pass for bert-ner: drive every control on mobile+desktop, confirm tap targets/focus/output, then flip support to ok (or unsupported+evidence).
Deeper mobile tap-target + focus-order audit on BGE-M3 (search input/button and the inspect select at 360px) to flip support from needs-review to ok with recorded evidence.
Add a BGE-M3 long-document chunk-vs-whole demo contrasting embedding a full section (8192 window) against sentence-chunking, to make the long-context advantage concrete.
Re-check upstream for a SciBERT scivocab masked-LM ONNX export; if one appears, build scibert-fill-mask (currently blocked with evidence) to show the scivocab-tokenization angle.
Add a domain-specific downstream demo (clinical NER or clinical sentence retrieval) that reuses the Bio_ClinicalBERT encoder, showing the same weights power more than fill-mask.
Add a 'live redaction' wild demo: mask identifying spans (Age/Sex/Date/person) as the user types, entirely on-device, foregrounding the de-identification privacy story.
Agent keyboard-traversal pass on biomedical-ner ladder pages (practical/wild/multi-model): confirm every control is reachable with visible focus on mobile+desktop.
Multi-model composition: biomedical NER → link extracted disease/drug spans to a small on-device ontology/embedding index for concept normalisation to a coding scheme.

Add a NEW demo for a fine-tuned byte-level model (spelling correction / transliteration / grapheme-to-phoneme with a real browser ONNX) to show ByT5's noise-robustness on a task the base checkpoint can't do — additive slug, never replacing this page.
Re-check InternVL (blocked): if Transformers.js adds an InternVL model class + processor and a canonical onnx-community ONNX export ships, build the on-device VLM demo.
Agent matrix pass for byt5-byte-level: Read the mobile+desktop screenshots, confirm tap targets + focus order + light/dark contrast, then flip support to ok.

Agent matrix pass for clip-zero-shot-image: Read the mobile+desktop screenshots, confirm real output + tap targets + focus + dialogs, then flip support to ok (or unsupported+evidence) and enrich this critique.
Time the multi-model retrieve→TinyLlama-explain path on a real constrained mobile device and record RTF/latency; consider a smaller explainer LLM option if WASM generation is too slow.
Add a Wild rung variant that clusters a whole snippet bank by intent (k-means over code embeddings) to visualise language-agnostic code families.
Agent matrix pass for codebert-fill-mask is done (desktop real-output run + mobile/desktop light+dark screenshots read); support flipped to ok. Re-audit if the design system changes.

Add a NEW additive demo for a language-specialised code MLM (e.g. onnx-community/codebert-javascript-ONNX) to contrast per-language vs multi-language code models (new slug, never replacing this page).
Add a WebGPU execution-provider fast path for convnext-classification with an honest WASM fallback.

Add a ConvNeXt-vs-ViT texture-vs-shape side-by-side: apply the same transform to one image and show both architectures' confidence collapse together (probes the ConvNet texture-bias vs transformer shape-bias claim directly).
